message:
refactor(core): migrate direct tokio task API calls to runtime_compat across 10 modules

Systematic migration of direct tokio task-spawning and join-type imports
to the runtime_compat abstraction layer, enabling transparent dual-runtime
support (tokio default / asupersync-runtime feature flag) without per-callsite
cfg blocks.

Changes per module:

- metrics.rs: tokio::task::JoinHandle → runtime_compat::task::JoinHandle;
  two tokio::spawn calls (metrics server accept loop, per-connection handler)
  → crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn.

- native_events.rs: tokio::task::JoinSet → runtime_compat::task::JoinSet;
  four tokio::spawn calls in tests → task::spawn.

- orphan_reaper.rs: Collapsed dual #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] /
  #[cfg(not(...))] spawn_blocking blocks into a single
  crate::runtime_compat::spawn_blocking call, preserving the error-wrapping
  fallback on JoinError.

- runtime.rs: tokio::task::{JoinHandle, JoinSet} imports replaced with
  runtime_compat::task equivalents; twelve tokio::spawn callsites across
  spawn_capture_engines, spawn_event_router, spawn_maintenance_task,
  spawn_heartbeat_task, spawn_tailer_supervisor, spawn_idle_capture_task,
  spawn_native_event_listener (outer + inner accept handle),
  spawn_spsc_drain_task, and spawn_persistence_pipeline all migrated to
  task::spawn.

- session_retention.rs: cleanup_sessions_async collapsed from dual cfg
  blocks (33 lines) into a single runtime_compat::spawn_blocking call
  (9 lines), eliminating code duplication while preserving identical
  behavior and error mapping. Doc comment updated to reference
  runtime_compat::spawn_blocking instead of tokio::task::spawn_blocking.

- tailer.rs: tokio::task::JoinSet → runtime_compat::task::JoinSet in both
  the main module and its #[cfg(test)] block.

- vendored/mux_client.rs: Added task import from runtime_compat; one
  tokio::spawn in subscribe_pane_output → task::spawn.

- watchdog.rs: tokio::task::JoinHandle → runtime_compat::task::JoinHandle;
  two tokio::spawn calls (spawn_watchdog, spawn_mux_watchdog) → task::spawn;
  get_mux_server_rss() collapsed from dual cfg spawn_blocking blocks into
  single runtime_compat::spawn_blocking call.

- web.rs: Added task import; tokio::task::JoinHandle → task::JoinHandle
  in WebServerHandle struct; three tokio::spawn calls (stream_events,
  stream_deltas, start_web_server) → task::spawn.

- workflows.rs: One tokio::spawn in WorkflowRunner dispatch →
  crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn.

No behavioral changes. All code paths produce identical results regardless
of which runtime backend is active.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
